revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伊藤正敏]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1955|birth_place={{ill|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|historian|qid=Q201788|1=Historian}}|native_name=伊藤正敏|given_name={{ill|Masatoshi|qid=Q6782732|1=Masatoshi}}}}
{{Other people|Masatoshi Itō|Gan Masatoshi}}
'''Masatoshi Itō''' (伊藤 正敏, born {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}}) is a Japanese historian specializing in the history of medieval Japan.
== Biography == Itō was born in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|qid=Q208863|lt=Hachioji, Tokyo|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. He graduated from the Faculty of Letters, Department of Japanese History at {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|qid=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}} and completed his master’s degree at the university’s Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology.
He worked as a cultural property investigator at the {{ill|Ichijodani Asakura Clan Ruins Research Institute|ja|一乗谷朝倉氏遺跡調査研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijodani Asakura Clan Ruins Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technical officer at the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}, and later served as an associate professor and then professor at {{ill|Nagaoka Institute of Design|en|Nagaoka Institute of Design|qid=Q11652156|lt=Nagaoka Institute of Design|1=Nagaoka Institute of Design}} before dedicating himself fully to research and writing. His fields of study include documentary history, archaeology, and cultural heritage administration. His research primarily focuses on the history of Japanese villages and the role of medieval {{ill|Temple and Shrine Powers|ja|寺社勢力|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple and Shrine Powers|qq=}}. *
== See Also == * {{ill|Kenmitsu System|ja|顕密体制|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmitsu System|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kenmon System Theory|ja|権門体制|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmon System Theory|qq=}}
* {{ill|Right of Asylum|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right of Asylum|qq=}}
* {{ill|Temple Precinct Cities|ja|境内都市|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple Precinct Cities|qq=}}
* {{ill|Jinaimachi|fr|Jinaicho|ja|寺内町|lt=Teramachi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinaimachi|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sōhei|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhei|qq=}}
* {{ill|Jinin|ja|神人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinin|qq=}}
== Publications == * *Late Medieval Villages: The Case of Kata Manor in Kii Province* (中世後期の村落 紀伊国賀太荘の場合), Yoshikawa Kobunkan, March 1991. * *Medieval Temple and Shrine Powers and Precinct Cities* (中世の寺社勢力と境内都市), Yoshikawa Kobunkan, May 1999. * *Medieval Japanese Temples: Forgotten Free Cities* (日本の中世寺院 忘れられた自由都市), Yoshikawa Kobunkan, *Rekishi Bunka Library*, February 2000. * *The Medieval Temple and Shrine Powers: Mu’en, Yu’en, and Migrants* (寺社勢力の中世 無縁・有縁・移民), Chikuma Shinsho, August 2008. * *The Mu’en Places of the Mi11le Ages* (無縁所の中世), Chikuma Shinsho, May 2010. * *Asylums and the State* (アジールと国家), Chikuma Sensho, February 2020.
== References == * Author profile from Chikuma Shinsho.
*
{{Authority control}} {{People-stub}}
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * * {{ill|Itō Masatoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Masatoshi|qq=}}
. *The Medieval Religious Powers: Migration and Connection.* Chikuma Shinsho, 2008.
